channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As he prepares to leave office, Biden urges incoming Democratic lawmakers to reach across the aisle
item.description: President Joe Biden is calling on incoming Democratic lawmakers to always be mindful of other people's perspectives — even when they may be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Higher Social Security payments coming for millions of people from bill that Biden signed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday signed into law a measure that boosts Social Security payments for current and former public employees, affecting nearly 3 million people who receive pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and in other public service jobs.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump to debut documentary film on Amazon in 2025
item.description: “As a private person, who has often been the subject of public scrutiny and misrepresentation, I feel a responsibility to clarify the facts,” First Lady Melania Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Legacy of Jan. 6 prosecutions in flux with Trump presidency on horizon
item.description: The Justice Department’s prosecution of the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack — hailed as one of the “largest, most complex, and most resource-intensive investigations” in its history — could soon go out with a fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Gingrich warns Freedom Caucus to study his era as conservatives issue demand letter following Johnson vote
item.description: Newt Gingrich engineered the Contract With America after forming the Conservative Opportunity Society, which some compare to today's Freedom Caucus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers brace for Trump's promised Jan. 6 pardons. Some are urging restraint
item.description: The fourth anniversary of the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol has a new focus as lawmakers brace for the prospect that Donald Trump may soon pardon many of the more than 1,500 people charged with crimes for their actions related to the riot.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No Jan. 6 disruptions are expected as Trump's win boosts Republicans' faith in elections -- for now
item.description: Four years ago, then-President Donald Trump rallied his supporters to march to the U.S. Capitol and urged his vice president to stop the congressional certification of Democrat Joe Biden's victory.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump appears with Italian Prime Minister Meloni at his Florida club
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump made an appearance Saturday with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, who was visiting his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's decision to block Nippon Steel takeover creates uncertainty for U.S. Steel workers
item.description: By blocking a Japanese company’s takeover of U.S. Steel, President Joe Biden says he is protecting good jobs in the American heartland.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman And Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: Judge Royce Lamberth denied the request in a Friday court order, reflecting on the event that took place after the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Outrage as Biden set to award Hillary Clinton, George Soros with Presidential Medal of Freedom
item.description: President Biden will on Saturday award the Presidential Medal of Freedom to former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, left-wing billionaire George Soros, and 17 others at a ceremony at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson held onto his job, but there are signs of trouble ahead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — This time was supposed to be different. But while House Speaker Mike Johnson on Friday avoided the dayslong ordeal that his predecessor, Kevin McCarthy, endured to become speaker, his relatively swift victory was hardly a unifying moment.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans attack came as officials warned of an escalating threat of international terrorism
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After Hamas launched the deadly assault on Israel that triggered retaliatory airstrikes on Gaza, FBI Director Christopher Wray said he feared the Middle East violence could embolden individuals or groups to carry out attacks inside the United States.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's sentencing is set for Jan. 10. Here's what could happen next
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Faced with the never-before-seen dilemma of how, when or even whether to sentence a former and future U.S. president, the judge in President-elect Donald Trump 's hush money case made a dramatic decision that could nevertheless bring the case to a muted end.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Excerpts from the judge's ruling to go ahead with Trump sentencing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's hush money case in New York dealt with a host of weighty, unprecedented questions before deciding Friday that the president-elect should be sentenced later this month for the crime of falsifying business records.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 119th Congress Latest: Mike Johnson narrowly reelected House speaker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The 119th Congress is convening for the first time and House Speaker Mike Johnson has narrowly been reelected to his post.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal courts will not refer allegations that Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas may have violated ethics laws to the Justice Department, the judiciary's policymaking body said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Mike Johnson wins Speakership on first ballot after 2 'no' votes flip
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) succeeded in keeping his gavel for the 119th Congress, winning in the House's Speakership vote in one round Friday. Johnson, who had a one-vote margin of error, won 218-215 over Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.). Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Here’s what Congress’s first day will look like
item.description: The 119th Congress kicks off Friday as lawmakers return to Washington with a long January to-do list and a packed first day. Confirmation hearings for President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees are set to get rolling in the coming weeks, and Trump himself will be inaugurated in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: New Year's attacks stoke fears of political violence
item.description: A pair of fatal attacks on New Year's Day have reignited concerns over political violence in the U.S. after an election already marked by multiple assassination attempts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Conservatives seek commitments as Johnson scrambles for Speaker votes
item.description: Roughly a dozen hard-line House conservatives are demanding a host of amorphous commitments from Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to win their support Friday, throwing his leadership tenure into uncertainty less than 24 hours before the vote.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards the 2nd highest civilian award to leaders of the Jan. 6 committee and 18 others
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday awarded the second highest civilian medal to Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill would exempt Tennessee SROs from immigration status reporting requirement
item.description: A new bill sponsored by a Republican senator would shield school resource officers from having to report a student's immigration status to the federal government in hopes of protecting children in schools.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: All Democrats expected to participate in Speaker’s vote
item.description: All Democrats are expected to be in the Capitol on Friday for the vote to seat the next Speaker of the House, according to the Democratic whip’s office.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Progressives face an existential crisis under Trump
item.description: Progressives in Washington are facing an existential crisis like never before as they brace for the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: New laws in NJ impact minimum wage, data privacy, and reading content
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- With every new year come new laws -- each impacting someone somewhere in the Garden State. New Jersey's minimum wage is increasing to $15.49 an hour for most workers.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Former Virginia GOP congressman forms exploratory committee for possible statewide run
item.description: Former Virginia Republican Rep. Denver Riggleman said he has formed an exploratory committee to run for statewide office as an Independent.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things that will shape US politics in 2025
item.description: The fallout of last month’s high-stakes election and recent battles within Congress are offering early clues of what will set the tone for politics in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Trump gives complete and total endorsement of Johnson as Speaker
item.description: President-elect Trump offered his complete and total endorsement of Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, days before a House vote on Friday to elect a new Speaker. "Speaker Mike Johnson is a good, hard working, religious man. He will do the right thing, and we will continue to WIN.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC
